text:
There was a small lake nestled in a serene valley. Birds sang throughout the morning as mist rose from its waters. A cheerful little duck waddled through the reeds, always happy and chirpy.

One chilly afternoon, an old, wise owl landed on a sturdy oak tree. She hooted softly to the ducks, exchanging news of the forest.

"Hello there," she hooted. "Could you tell me a nice story?"

The ducks fluffed their feathers, eager to share their tales. "There was once a wise old turtle..."

"The wise old turtle," interrupted a chirpy little warbler. "Once upon a time, there was a wise old turtle..."

"In a pond not too far away lived a wise old turtle," interrupted yet another. "He was known for his wisdom and his magical powers to turn rocks into mice."

"And he always said," hooted a soft voice, "that the most precious treasure in the world was friendship."

The little ducks agreed
